activities_projects.title: Water Sources Map Activity
activities_projects.objective: Identify major rivers, lakes, and groundwater sources in India
steps: Take a blank political map of India
Mark major rivers, lakes, and groundwater sources
Write one important fact about each source
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn the geographical distribution of water sources in India

activities_projects.title: Rainwater Harvesting Model
steps: Cut a plastic bottle and prepare a simple model showing rainwater collection
Use sand, clay, and stones to filter water
Demonstrate how water can be stored and used
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand practical ways to conserve water

activities_projects.title: Daily Water Usage Diary
steps: Record all water used in a day at home or school
Categorize usage as drinking, cleaning, bathing, or gardening
Reflect on how to reduce water wastage
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students become aware of water usage and conservation methods

activities_projects.title: Water Pollution Poster
activities_projects.objective: Learn about causes and effects of water pollution
steps: Choose one type of water pollution (industrial, domestic, or agricultural)
Create a poster showing its causes and effects
Suggest ways to reduce water pollution
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand water pollution and its impact

activities_projects.title: Experiment: Water Filtration
activities_projects.objective: Observe how water can be purified
steps: Cut the bottle and layer cotton, sand, and gravel inside
Pour dirty water through the filter
Observe and note the changes in water clarity
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn basic water filtration techniques

activities_projects.title: Comparison of Rivers Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand differences in river sources and uses
steps: Select two rivers from different regions of India
Compare their sources, length, and usage (irrigation, drinking, transport)
Write a short summary of similarities and differences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand regional diversity of rivers and their importance
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and completeness of comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Writing: A Day Without Water
steps: Imagine a day without any water at home or school
Write a short story describing challenges faced
Suggest solutions to conserve water
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand importance of water in daily life
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance to chapter concepts (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Water Conservation Slogan Activity
activities_projects.objective: Promote water conservation through creative expression
steps: Create five slogans promoting water conservation
Illustrate them with small drawings or symbols
Share slogans with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students express understanding of water conservation in a creative way
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and clarity of message (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mini Quiz on Water
steps: Prepare 10 simple questions on water sources, uses, and conservation
Ask classmates to answer them
Keep score for correct answers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students strengthen knowledge through peer learning

activities_projects.title: Field Visit: Local Water Body
activities_projects.objective: Observe a real water source and its uses
steps: Visit a nearby pond, lake, or river
Observe and note its uses and condition
Discuss with classmates ways to protect it
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect theoretical knowledge with real-life observation
